en.extract:
The Mussau triller (Lalage conjuncta) is a smaller member of the cuckooshrike family, Campephagidae.  It was formerly considered a subspecies of the varied triller. It is found on the St Matthias Islands in the Bismarck Archipelago. Photographed and sound-recorded for the first time in 2024, the species is now thought to be restricted to the centre of the island and may be at risk due to logging.


References

Taylor, P.B. 2005. Family Campephagidae (cuckoo-shrikes). Pages 40–122 in J. del Hoyo, A. Elliott, and D.A. Christie (editors), Handbook of the birds of the world. Volume 10. Cuckoo-shrikes to thrushes. Lynx Edicions. Barcelona.

The '''Mussau triller''' ('''''Lalage conjuncta''''') is a smaller member of the [[cuckooshrike]] family, Campephagidae.  It was formerly considered a subspecies of the [[varied triller]]. It is found on the [[St Matthias Islands]] in the [[Bismarck Archipelago]]. Photographed and sound-recorded for the first time in 2024,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bergmark |first=Joshua |date=2025-02-10 |title=Ornis Birding Expeditions Trip Reports (Papua New Guinea: Islands 8th June - 28th June 2024 Led by Joshua Bergmark) |url=https://www.ornis-birding.com/trip-reports |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20241010081242/https://www.ornis-birding.com/trip-reports |archive-date=2024-10-10 |access-date=2025-02-10 |website=Ornis Birding Expeditions}}</ref> the species is now thought to be restricted to the centre of the island and may be at risk due to logging.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Sawatzky |first=Katie Doke |date=2024-08-27 |title=FOUND! Mussau Triller rediscovered in Papua New Guinea |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/news/massau-triller |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240924015113/https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/news/massau-triller |archive-date=2024-09-24 |access-date=2025-02-10 |work=Cornell Lab Birds of the World News |pages=1}}</ref>
